messages.text: That might be a slight exaggeration power is in the range of 1 to 100K kilowatts. A list of the commercial radio stations and their power outputs can be found here https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cougar_Mountain (edited)
messages.content_html: That might be a slight exaggeration power is in the range of 1 to 100K kilowatts. A list of the commercial radio stations and their power outputs can be found here https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cougar_Mountain (edited)
embeds_html:
Cougar Mountain
Cougar Mountain is a peak in the Issaquah Alps in King County, Washington. It is part of the highlands in the Eastside suburbs of Seattle, and at 1,614 feet (492 m) it is the lowest and westernmost of the Alps. About two-thirds of Cougar Mountain has experienced residential development, and is home to many neighborhood communities such as Lakemo...
messages.reply_html:
Cisien
The red/white tower in the background of the first image here is a high power FM radio transmitter -- I'm told it transmits over 1 megawatt of power combined among the stations it supports

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: 130 miles, 209 KM straight line. But there are mountains in-between so it told a zigzag route to get from UBC to our main Cougar mountain repeater. I think it went from UBC to @ded (SalishMesh) repeater on Mt Work on Vancouver Island in Victoria, then to Mt Erie, then to Cougar. The routed path is around 157 miles, 252 KM.
messages.content_html: 130 miles, 209 KM straight line. But there are mountains in-between so it told a zigzag route to get from UBC to our main Cougar mountain repeater. I think it went from UBC to @ded (SalishMesh) repeater on Mt Work on Vancouver Island in Victoria, then to Mt Erie, then to Cougar. The routed path is around 157 miles, 252 KM.

Ok How far is the Vancouver Repeater away?

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: some context: 1. 0:00: overall view of the current Seattle MeshCore repeater coverage. You can see repeaters as far south as Tacoma 25 miles, 41KM LOS to Cougar Mountain 2. 0 zoom in to our main Cougar Mountain repeater that 500M, 1600ft up in the mountain with clear view to Seattle. 3. 0:22: then zoom into Seattle downtown with lots of high-rise office, condominium and apartment buildings. There are 8 to 9 repeaters serving the downtown area with exceptional coverage. You really don't need cellular network there to communicate. all you need is meshcore. 4. 0:28: pan up north to see more repeaters in North Seattle and suburbs north of Seattle. 5: 0:43 There are 3 repeaters 70 miles, 113 KM LOS to Cougar Mountain. The 2 repeaters on Mount Erie are the link to Victoria on Vancouver Island. 6: 0:51: Reveals Vancouver Island and the repeaters on Vancouver Island. MeshCore crossing international border from Mt Work on Vancouver Island to the Erie repeaters. (disclaimer: the UBC repeater was a temp repeater setup to test range to Victoria. The test was a great success and it will be setup permanently later) 7: 1 Surprise! Even City of Vancouver is linked up! 130 miles, 209 KM away from Cougar Mountain. 8: 1:18 Zoom back out the show the whole region including City of Vancouver, Victoria, and the rest of Puget Sound. @ded (SalishMesh) and @Cisien can chime in and correct my description.
messages.content_html: some context: 1. 0:00: overall view of the current Seattle MeshCore repeater coverage. You can see repeaters as far south as Tacoma 25 miles, 41KM LOS to Cougar Mountain 2. 0 zoom in to our main Cougar Mountain repeater that 500M, 1600ft up in the mountain with clear view to Seattle. 3. 0:22: then zoom into Seattle downtown with lots of high-rise office, condominium and apartment buildings. There are 8 to 9 repeaters serving the downtown area with exceptional coverage. You really don't need cellular network there to communicate. all you need is meshcore. 4. 0:28: pan up north to see more repeaters in North Seattle and suburbs north of Seattle. 5: 0:43 There are 3 repeaters 70 miles, 113 KM LOS to Cougar Mountain. The 2 repeaters on Mount Erie are the link to Victoria on Vancouver Island. 6: 0:51: Reveals Vancouver Island and the repeaters on Vancouver Island. MeshCore crossing international border from Mt Work on Vancouver Island to the Erie repeaters. (disclaimer: the UBC repeater was a temp repeater setup to test range to Victoria. The test was a great success and it will be setup permanently later) 7: 1 Surprise! Even City of Vancouver is linked up! 130 miles, 209 KM away from Cougar Mountain. 8: 1:18 Zoom back out the show the whole region including City of Vancouver, Victoria, and the rest of Puget Sound. @ded (SalishMesh) and @Cisien can chime in and correct my description.

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: I think the "naysayers" will say oh this is just tropo ducting, and it is true. But it took 10 hops to make the trip. the other mesh would not have made this same trip even with tropo ducting. The point is, if there is a hop to be made, meshcore will make it, up to 64 hops.
messages.content_html: I think the "naysayers" will say oh this is just tropo ducting, and it is true. But it took 10 hops to make the trip. the other mesh would not have made this same trip even with tropo ducting. The point is, if there is a hop to be made, meshcore will make it, up to 64 hops.
messages.reactions_html: 3

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: I've tried to explain that too, like do you want your laptop and cellphone routing traffic in your WiFi mesh?
messages.content_html: I've tried to explain that too, like do you want your laptop and cellphone routing traffic in your WiFi mesh?
messages.reactions_html: 2
messages.reply_html:
Uncle Lit
I have also read some people lament that meshcore is not a real mesh becuase companions (they think clients) don't repeat. They are wrong. meshcore repeaters together make the mesh. there is no funny business of managed flood. if a repeater hears it, it WILL repeater it. "repeaters is the mesh", the 64 hop limit, and companions don't auto spam the mesh are some of the reasons why this trip took place.

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: hi @andykirby , any update on the puget sound mesh show case video?
messages.content_html: hi @andykirby, any update on the puget sound mesh show case video?

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: yes, as Cisien said, we are all on narrow, aka BW 62.5, SF7 and 910.525MHz. it has been working so well that ded in Victoria, BC is using it and is part of our mesh regularly, Vancouver BC mainland is switching over, Portland, Oregon has switched over to narrow and they have been getting our messages through tropo ducting. Boston, MA is also on narrow and are having a great experience. @MrAlders0n and their meshcore mesh in Ottawa are building out their mesh on narrow I think. These cities and regions I mentioned are the biggest mesh networks in North America.
messages.content_html: yes, as Cisien said, we are all on narrow, aka BW 62.5, SF7 and 910.525MHz. it has been working so well that ded in Victoria, BC is using it and is part of our mesh regularly, Vancouver BC mainland is switching over, Portland, Oregon has switched over to narrow and they have been getting our messages through tropo ducting. Boston, MA is also on narrow and are having a great experience. @MrAlders0n and their meshcore mesh in Ottawa are building out their mesh on narrow I think. These cities and regions I mentioned are the biggest mesh networks in North America.
messages.reply_html:
andykirby
Hey Man haha no I haven't had the chance, just got really busy after this latest video and working on our mesh here with new users etc! It's on the list i think im good for material lol! One thing I did want to check, are you all running narrow settings now? (edited)

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: yes, very good point!
messages.content_html: yes, very good point!
messages.reply_html:
awol
and it's hard to think of populated areas in the US that are more geographically challenged than the PNW for mesh networks so if it's working well here, should be pretty good (edited)

messages.author: MrAlders0n
messages.text: Hey! We are have now migrated to narrow and we are having excellent success. We’re on the old settings until about 3-4 weeks ago. We are all addicted to the speed ahaha. If you folks have any questions let me know! We are growing rapidly as of late
messages.content_html: Hey! We are have now migrated to narrow and we are having excellent success. We’re on the old settings until about 3-4 weeks ago. We are all addicted to the speed ahaha. If you folks have any questions let me know! We are growing rapidly as of late
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: MrAlders0n
messages.text: We are still working to convert our last few MT folks @Uncle Lit had a dose of that in our discord today
messages.content_html: We are still working to convert our last few MT folks @Uncle Lit had a dose of that in our discord today

messages.author: MrAlders0n
messages.text: I will say with narrow we have one connection that was far using a yagi that doesn’t seem to reach as reliably anymore but we are substituting that with more repeaters. It just works faster and better on narrow we find.
messages.content_html: I will say with narrow we have one connection that was far using a yagi that doesn’t seem to reach as reliably anymore but we are substituting that with more repeaters. It just works faster and better on narrow we find.

messages.author: Jade
messages.text: Hey @andykirby Here's the video from Kremwerk, the world's first MeshCore equipped nightclub complex, taken last Saturday As you can see the queue to get in was around the block for a monthly night they have called Saizon. I spotted a couple of people with T-decks and another person with a T1000e Kremwerk repeater specs are as follows: PeakMesh RAK 4631 Solar Repeater, Rokland 6db antenna. 4K version - https://drive.google.com/file/d/139A8VHbtiabnt2slNrCrPBq1L-wFfcfn/view?usp=sharing 1080 version - https://drive.google.com/file/d/10nfSOil45brtU_ORuO609YZvg_rT2xYy/view?usp=sharing If you have any questions or need context for any of the shots let me know. You may want to mute and replace the songs audio if you use it to avoid any issues with music on Youtube.
